module std.cast

/**
 * @file std/cast
 *
 * Type-cast intrinsics (`bridge func` — compiler-provided, bodyless). The
 * compiler recognises them by name and lowers calls directly. Each has surface
 * sugar:
 *
 * - `std::cast<T>(x)`    ≡ `x as T`   — converting/static cast (also `x.toString` for String).
 * - `std::dyncast<T>(x)` ≡ `x as? T`  — runtime-checked downcast to `T?`.
 * - `std::bitcast<T>(x)` ≡ `x as* T`  — bit reinterpretation (requires `T.sizeOf == U.sizeOf`).
 *
 * @since 0.0.4
 */

friend zone std {
    /** Converting cast (`static_cast`). Numeric conversions, stringify, up/down casts. */
    @SinceAzora("0.0.4")
    bridge func<T, U> cast(value: U): T

    /** Runtime-checked downcast (`dynamic_cast`); null when `value` is not a `T`. */
    @SinceAzora("0.0.4")
    bridge func<T, U> dyncast(value: U): T?

    /**
     * Bit reinterpretation (`reinterpret_cast`). Requires `T.sizeOf == U.sizeOf`;
     * the compiler enforces this at the lowering site.
     */
    @SinceAzora("0.0.4")
    bridge func<T, U> bitcast(value: U): T
}

module std.concurrency.coroutines

/**
 * @file std/concurrency/coroutines
 *
 * Coroutine-based concurrency primitives.
 *
 * Provides task-based utilities for asynchronous execution,
 * including delayed actions.
 *
 * @since 0.0.1
 */

friend zone std {
    /**
     * Executes an action after a specified delay.
     *
     * Suspends for the given number of milliseconds, then invokes the action.
     *
     * @param ms The delay in milliseconds before executing the action.
     * @param action The function to execute after the delay.
     */
    @Experimental(sinceAzora: "0.0.1")
    task delayed(ms: Int, action: () -> Unit) {
        suspend ms
        action()
    }
}

module std.convert

export import std.convert.core

friend zone std {
    /**
     * Declares conversion into `T`.
     *
     * Implementing `Into<String>` for a type creates a property-style member
     * named from the `use as` template. For `T = String`, this spec exposes
     * `.toString` without parentheses.
     *
     * @param T target type produced by the conversion.
     */
    @Experimental(sinceAzora: "0.0.3")
    spec Into<T>: T (ref self) use as "to${T.typeName}"

    /**
     * Declares conversion from `T`.
     *
     * Implementing `From<String>` for a type creates a property-style member
     * named from the `use as` template. For `T = String`, this spec exposes
     * `.fromString` without parentheses.
     *
     * @param T source type represented by the conversion.
     */
    @Experimental(sinceAzora: "0.0.3")
    spec From<T>: T (ref self) use as "from${T.typeName}"
}

    bridge deco UncheckedCast for [.Func, .Prop, .Task, .Flow, .Var, .Fin, .ImplOper]

module std.io

/**
 * @file std/io/io
 *
 * Standard I/O formatting utilities.
 *
 * Provides common formatting constants and helper functions
 * for text output such as separators and section headers.
 *
 * @since 0.0.1
 */

friend zone std {
    friend zone fmt {
        /** A newline character. */
        @SinceAzora("0.0.1")
        fin newline = "\n"

        /** A tab character. */
        @SinceAzora("0.0.1")
        fin tab = "\t"

        /** A single space character. */
        @SinceAzora("0.0.1")
        fin space = " "
    }

    /**
     * Writes `value` to standard output without appending a newline.
     *
     * This compiler bridge uses the same value formatting as `println`.
     * Consecutive calls continue on the same output line.
     *
     * @param value The value to write.
     */
    @SinceAzora("0.0.4")
    bridge func print(value: Any): Unit

    /**
     * Console output intrinsic.
     *
     * A real, importable bridge whose implementation is supplied by each
     * backend and the interpreter. Reach as `std::println` after `import std.io`.
     * Accepts `Any` so it prints every type; the backend dispatches on the
     * argument's concrete type.
     */
    /** Prints `value` followed by a newline. */
    @SinceAzora("0.0.1")
    bridge func println(value: Any): Unit

    /**
     * Creates a separator string by repeating a character.
     *
     * @param char The character or string to repeat.
     * @param length The number of times to repeat it.
     * @return A string of the given character repeated length times.
     */
    @Experimental(sinceAzora: "0.0.1")
    func separator(char: String, length: Int): String = char * length

    /**
     * Prints a formatted section header with surrounding separator lines.
     *
     * @param title The header text to display.
     * @param width The width of the separator lines.
     * @return The title string.
     */
    @Experimental(sinceAzora: "0.0.1")
    func header(title: String, width: Int): String {
        var line: String = separator("-", width)
        println(line)
        println(title)
        println(line)
        return title
    }
}

module std.reactive

friend zone std {

}

/* * Copyright 2026 AzoraLabs * * Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License"); * you may not use this file except in compliance with the License. * You may obtain a copy of the License at * * http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0 * * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and * limitations under the License. */ module std.reflection /** * @file std/reflection * * The compile-time reflection intrinsic. `std::reflect` yields a compile-time * handle to the declaration named `X` (a type, a value binding, or a `Type::member` * path). It is used as a prop — no call parentheses — and is only valid in * compile-time positions: * * ``` * std::reflect

.hasDeco // Bool * std::reflect

.decoMeta.value // decorator field * std::reflect

.zone?.label // enclosing zone metadata * ``` * * The compiler lowers every `std::reflect` at parse time, so the declaration * below is a placeholder that is never executed. * * @since 0.0.4 */ friend zone std { /** * Compile-time reflection handle for the declaration `T`, produced by * `std::reflect`. Its members (`.hasDeco`, `.decoMeta`, `.zone`) are * resolved by the compiler; `bridge pack` means no struct is emitted. */ @Experimental(sinceAzora: "0.0.4") bridge pack Reflected /** * Compile-time reflection handle for the declaration `T`. Used as a prop * (`std::reflect`). `includeInherited` also surfaces decorators inherited * from base declarations when set. */ @Experimental(sinceAzora: "0.0.4") bridge func reflect(includeInherited: Bool = false): Reflected }

export module std.string.core

use friend zone std {
    @SinceAzora("0.0.4")
    bridge pack String
}

// Azora Engine — Metal shader source (MSL), compiled at startup by az_gpu.az.
//
// 3D: interleaved position+normal vertices, per-draw viewProj/model/color/light
//     uniforms, simple configurable directional lambert shading.
// 2D: position+uv+color vertices in physical pixels mapped to NDC, optional
//     texture (text quads) modulated by the vertex color.

module engine.shaders

/** Complete Metal shader library containing the equivalent 3D and 2D stages. */
func metalShaderSource(): String {
    return (""
        + "#include <metal_stdlib>\n"
        + "using namespace metal;\n"
        + "struct V3Out { float4 pos [[position]]; float3 normal; };\n"
        + "struct U3 { float4x4 viewProj; float4x4 model; float4 color; float4 light; };\n"
        + "vertex V3Out v3_main(uint vid [[vertex_id]],\n"
        + "                     const device float* verts [[buffer(0)]],\n"
        + "                     constant U3& u [[buffer(1)]]) {\n"
        + "    V3Out out;\n"
        + "    float3 p = float3(verts[vid*6+0], verts[vid*6+1], verts[vid*6+2]);\n"
        + "    float3 n = float3(verts[vid*6+3], verts[vid*6+4], verts[vid*6+5]);\n"
        + "    out.pos = u.viewProj * u.model * float4(p, 1.0);\n"
        + "    out.normal = (u.model * float4(n, 0.0)).xyz;\n"
        + "    return out;\n"
        + "}\n"
        + "fragment float4 f3_main(V3Out in [[stage_in]], constant U3& u [[buffer(1)]]) {\n"
        + "    float3 l = normalize(u.light.xyz);\n"
        + "    float d = max(dot(normalize(in.normal), l), 0.0);\n"
        + "    float ambient = clamp(u.light.w, 0.0, 1.0);\n"
        + "    float i = ambient + (1.0 - ambient) * d;\n"
        + "    return float4(u.color.rgb * i, u.color.a);\n"
        + "}\n"
        + "struct V2Out { float4 pos [[position]]; float2 uv; float4 color; };\n"
        + "struct U2 { float2 viewport; float useTex; float pad; };\n"
        + "vertex V2Out v2_main(uint vid [[vertex_id]],\n"
        + "                     const device float* verts [[buffer(0)]],\n"
        + "                     constant U2& u [[buffer(1)]]) {\n"
        + "    V2Out out;\n"
        + "    float2 p = float2(verts[vid*8+0], verts[vid*8+1]);\n"
        + "    out.pos = float4(p.x / u.viewport.x * 2.0 - 1.0,\n"
        + "                     1.0 - p.y / u.viewport.y * 2.0, 0.0, 1.0);\n"
        + "    out.uv = float2(verts[vid*8+2], verts[vid*8+3]);\n"
        + "    out.color = float4(verts[vid*8+4], verts[vid*8+5], verts[vid*8+6], verts[vid*8+7]);\n"
        + "    return out;\n"
        + "}\n"
        + "fragment float4 f2_main(V2Out in [[stage_in]], constant U2& u [[buffer(1)]],\n"
        + "                        texture2d<float> tex [[texture(0)]],\n"
        + "                        sampler smp [[sampler(0)]]) {\n"
        + "    if (u.useTex > 0.5) {\n"
        + "        float4 t = tex.sample(smp, in.uv);\n"
        + "        return float4(in.color.rgb, in.color.a * t.a);\n"
        + "    }\n"
        + "    return in.color;\n"
        + "}\n"
    )
}

/** Compatibility name used by the native Metal renderer. */
func shaderSource(): String {
    return metalShaderSource()
}

/** Vulkan GLSL 4.50 equivalent of Metal `v3_main`. */
func vulkan3dVertexShaderSource(): String {
    return (""
        + "#version 450\n"
        + "layout(location = 0) in vec3 inPosition;\n"
        + "layout(location = 1) in vec3 inNormal;\n"
        + "layout(set = 0, binding = 0) uniform U3 { mat4 viewProj; mat4 model; vec4 color; vec4 light; } u;\n"
        + "layout(location = 0) out vec3 normal;\n"
        + "void main() {\n"
        + "    gl_Position = u.viewProj * u.model * vec4(inPosition, 1.0);\n"
        + "    normal = mat3(u.model) * inNormal;\n"
        + "}\n"
    )
}

/** Vulkan GLSL 4.50 equivalent of Metal `f3_main`. */
func vulkan3dFragmentShaderSource(): String {
    return (""
        + "#version 450\n"
        + "layout(location = 0) in vec3 normal;\n"
        + "layout(set = 0, binding = 0) uniform U3 { mat4 viewProj; mat4 model; vec4 color; vec4 light; } u;\n"
        + "layout(location = 0) out vec4 outColor;\n"
        + "void main() {\n"
        + "    vec3 l = normalize(u.light.xyz);\n"
        + "    float d = max(dot(normalize(normal), l), 0.0);\n"
        + "    float ambient = clamp(u.light.w, 0.0, 1.0);\n"
        + "    float intensity = ambient + (1.0 - ambient) * d;\n"
        + "    outColor = vec4(u.color.rgb * intensity, u.color.a);\n"
        + "}\n"
    )
}

/** Vulkan GLSL 4.50 equivalent of Metal `v2_main`. */
func vulkan2dVertexShaderSource(): String {
    return (""
        + "#version 450\n"
        + "layout(location = 0) in vec2 inPosition;\n"
        + "layout(location = 1) in vec2 inUv;\n"
        + "layout(location = 2) in vec4 inColor;\n"
        + "layout(set = 0, binding = 0) uniform U2 { vec2 viewport; float useTex; float pad; } u;\n"
        + "layout(location = 0) out vec2 uv;\n"
        + "layout(location = 1) out vec4 color;\n"
        + "void main() {\n"
        + "    gl_Position = vec4(inPosition.x / u.viewport.x * 2.0 - 1.0, 1.0 - inPosition.y / u.viewport.y * 2.0, 0.0, 1.0);\n"
        + "    uv = inUv;\n"
        + "    color = inColor;\n"
        + "}\n"
    )
}

/** Vulkan GLSL 4.50 equivalent of Metal `f2_main`. */
func vulkan2dFragmentShaderSource(): String {
    return (""
        + "#version 450\n"
        + "layout(location = 0) in vec2 uv;\n"
        + "layout(location = 1) in vec4 color;\n"
        + "layout(set = 0, binding = 0) uniform U2 { vec2 viewport; float useTex; float pad; } u;\n"
        + "layout(set = 0, binding = 1) uniform sampler2D tex;\n"
        + "layout(location = 0) out vec4 outColor;\n"
        + "void main() {\n"
        + "    if (u.useTex > 0.5) {\n"
        + "        vec4 sampled = texture(tex, uv);\n"
        + "        outColor = vec4(color.rgb, color.a * sampled.a);\n"
        + "    } else {\n"
        + "        outColor = color;\n"
        + "    }\n"
        + "}\n"
    )
}

/** WebGL 2 GLSL ES 3.00 equivalent of Metal `v3_main`. */
func webgl3dVertexShaderSource(): String {
    return (""
        + "#version 300 es\n"
        + "precision highp float;\n"
        + "in vec3 position;\n"
        + "in vec3 normal;\n"
        + "uniform mat4 modelViewMatrix;\n"
        + "uniform mat4 projectionMatrix;\n"
        + "uniform mat3 normalMatrix;\n"
        + "out vec3 vNormal;\n"
        + "void main() {\n"
        + "    gl_Position = projectionMatrix * modelViewMatrix * vec4(position, 1.0);\n"
        + "    vNormal = normalize(normalMatrix * normal);\n"
        + "}\n"
    )
}

/** WebGL 2 GLSL ES 3.00 equivalent of Metal `f3_main`. */
func webgl3dFragmentShaderSource(): String {
    return (""
        + "#version 300 es\n"
        + "precision highp float;\n"
        + "in vec3 vNormal;\n"
        + "uniform vec4 uColor;\n"
        + "uniform vec4 uLight;\n"
        + "out vec4 outColor;\n"
        + "void main() {\n"
        + "    float d = max(dot(normalize(vNormal), normalize(uLight.xyz)), 0.0);\n"
        + "    float ambient = clamp(uLight.w, 0.0, 1.0);\n"
        + "    float intensity = ambient + (1.0 - ambient) * d;\n"
        + "    outColor = vec4(uColor.rgb * intensity, uColor.a);\n"
        + "}\n"
    )
}

/** WebGL 2 GLSL ES 3.00 equivalent of Metal `v2_main`. */
func webgl2dVertexShaderSource(): String {
    return (""
        + "#version 300 es\n"
        + "precision highp float;\n"
        + "in vec3 position;\n"
        + "in vec2 uv;\n"
        + "in vec4 color;\n"
        + "uniform vec2 uViewport;\n"
        + "out vec2 vUv;\n"
        + "out vec4 vColor;\n"
        + "void main() {\n"
        + "    gl_Position = vec4(position.x / uViewport.x * 2.0 - 1.0, 1.0 - position.y / uViewport.y * 2.0, 0.0, 1.0);\n"
        + "    vUv = uv;\n"
        + "    vColor = color;\n"
        + "}\n"
    )
}

/** WebGL 2 GLSL ES 3.00 equivalent of Metal `f2_main`. */
func webgl2dFragmentShaderSource(): String {
    return (""
        + "#version 300 es\n"
        + "precision highp float;\n"
        + "in vec2 vUv;\n"
        + "in vec4 vColor;\n"
        + "uniform bool uUseTexture;\n"
        + "uniform sampler2D uTexture;\n"
        + "out vec4 outColor;\n"
        + "void main() {\n"
        + "    if (uUseTexture) {\n"
        + "        vec4 sampled = texture(uTexture, vUv);\n"
        + "        outColor = vec4(vColor.rgb, vColor.a * sampled.a);\n"
        + "    } else {\n"
        + "        outColor = vColor;\n"
        + "    }\n"
        + "}\n"
    )
}
